          eLpNorm (fun ω => X i ω + Y i ω) 2 P = eLpNorm (fun ω => X i ω + Y i ω) 2 P := rfl
          _ ≤ eLpNorm (X i) 2 P + eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P := by
            -- Apply the Minkowski inequality for L² norm
            apply eLpNorm.add_le _ _ (by norm_num)
      -- Take the supremum over i and use the fact that the norms are bounded
      have h₅₂ : ⨆ i, eLpNorm (fun ω => X i ω + Y i ω) 2 P ≤ ⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P + eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P := by
        exact iSup_le (fun i => h₅₁ i)
      have h₅₃ : ⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P + eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P < ∞ := by
        calc
          ⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P + eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P ≤ ⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P + ⨆ i, eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P := by
            -- Use the fact that the sum of sups is greater than or equal to the sup of sums
            calc
              ⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P + eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P ≤ ⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P + eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P := le_refl _
              _ ≤ ⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P + ⨆ i, eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P := by
                -- Use the fact that the sum of sups is greater than or equal to the sup of sums
                have h₅₄ : ∀ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P + eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P ≤ ⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P + ⨆ i, eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P := by
                  intro i
                  have h₅₅ : eLpNorm (X i) 2 P ≤ ⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P := le_iSup (fun i => eLpNorm (X i) 2 P) i
                  have h₅₆ : eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P ≤ ⨆ i, eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P := le_iSup (fun i => eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P) i
                  linarith
                exact le_iSup_of_le (fun i => h₅₄ i)
          _ < ∞ := by
            -- Use the fact that both sups are finite
            have h₅₇ : ⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P < ∞ := hX.bounded
            have h₅₈ : ⨆ i, eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P < ∞ := hY.bounded
            -- The sum of two finite values is finite
            have h₅₉ : (⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P : WithTop ℝ) < ∞ := by simpa using h₅₇
            have h₅₁₀ : (⨆ i, eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P : WithTop ℝ) < ∞ := by simpa using h₅₈
            -- Use the fact that the sum of two finite values is finite
            norm_num [add_lt_top] at h₅₉ h₅₁₀ ⊢ <;>
              (try norm_num) <;>
              (try simp_all [WithTop.coe_lt_coe]) <;>
              (try linarith) <;>
              (try
                {
                  cases' le_total (⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P : ℝ≥0∞) (⨆ i, eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P : ℝ≥0∞) with h h <;>
                    simp_all [WithTop.coe_lt_coe, add_comm] <;>
                    (try norm_num) <;>
                    (try linarith)
                }) <;>
              (try
                {
                  norm_num at h₅₉ h₅₁₀ ⊢ <;>
                    simp_all [WithTop.coe_lt_coe] <;>
                    (try linarith)
                })
      -- Combine the inequalities to get the final result
      calc
        ⨆ i, eLpNorm (fun ω => X i ω + Y i ω) 2 P ≤ ⨆ i, eLpNorm (X i) 2 P + eLpNorm (Y i) 2 P := h₅₂
        _ < ∞ := h₅₃
    -- Use the boundedness to conclude the proof
    simpa [h₃, h₄] using h₅

have hX_cadlag : IsCadlag (X · ω) := hX.cadlag ω
    have hY_cadlag : IsCadlag (Y · ω) := hY.cadlag ω
    have hSum_cadlag : IsCadlag (fun x => X x ω + Y x ω) := IsCadlag.add hX_cadlag hY_cadlag
    exact hSum_cadlag
  · sorry
